title: campanula axillaris
intro: Campanula axillaris, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Campanula axillaris are attributed to its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. Campanula axillaris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
